summary.zh: 《创世记》记载"神的儿子们"与人的女子所生的上古巨人，"古时英武有名的人"。《以诺一书》把他们写成堕落守望者的后裔，是大洪水的起因之一。
summary.en: Giants born of the 'sons of God' and the daughters of men in Genesis, 'the mighty men of old, men of renown.' In 1 Enoch they are offspring of the fallen Watchers, a cause of the Flood.

description.zh:
拿非利人出自《创世记》第六章开头四节经文，紧接在大洪水叙事之前：人在地上多起来，"神的儿子们"见人的女子美貌，随意挑选娶来为妻；那时候有拿非利人在地上，后来也有——神的儿子们与人的女子交合生子，"那就是上古英武有名的人"。经文简短而含混，希伯来语 nephilim 一般联系动词"坠落"（naphal）解作"坠落者"，七十士译本径译作"巨人"（gigantes）。《民数记》第十三章再次提及：窥探迦南的探子回报说在那里看见了亚衲族人，是拿非利人的后代，"我们看自己就如蚱蜢一样"。

公元前三至二世纪的《以诺一书·守望者之书》把这四节经文扩写成完整的堕落叙事：两百名称为"守望者"的天使在示米哈撒（Shemihazah）率领下于黑门山立誓下凡，娶人间女子，并教人冶金、兵器、化妆、法术与观星——阿撒泻勒（Azazel）传授的兵器与虚饰之术尤受谴责。他们生下身量巨大的拿非利人，巨人吃尽人的出产后开始吞食人畜、饮血，大地向天呼冤，神遂差米迦勒等天使长囚禁守望者，并以洪水涤荡大地。这一叙事在死海古卷（如《巨人书》残篇）与《禧年书》中均有平行版本，在第二圣殿时期流传极广。

后世犹太与基督教主流解经渐渐转向温和读法，把"神的儿子们"解作塞特的后裔而非天使；但守望者与巨人的故事从未消失，从中世纪传说一直流进现代奇幻小说、漫画与影视，nephilim 一词如今是半天使血统角色的通用标签。
description.en:
The Nephilim appear in the four verses that open Genesis 6, immediately before the Flood narrative: as humankind multiplied, the 'sons of God' saw that the daughters of men were beautiful and took wives of all they chose; the Nephilim were on the earth in those days — and afterward — when the sons of God came in to the daughters of men, who bore them children: 'these were the mighty men of old, the men of renown.' The passage is terse and cryptic. The Hebrew nephilim is usually linked to the verb naphal, 'to fall,' hence 'fallen ones'; the Septuagint simply renders it gigantes, giants. Numbers 13 mentions them again: the spies sent into Canaan report seeing the Anakim, descended from the Nephilim, 'and we seemed like grasshoppers to ourselves.'

The Book of the Watchers in 1 Enoch, written in the third or second century BCE, expands those verses into a full narrative of angelic fall: two hundred angels called Watchers, led by Shemihazah, swear an oath on Mount Hermon and descend to take human wives, teaching metallurgy, weapon-making, cosmetics, sorcery, and astrology — the arms and adornments taught by Azazel are singled out for blame. Their gigantic offspring devour the produce of humankind, then humans and beasts themselves, and drink blood; the earth cries out, God sends the archangels to imprison the Watchers, and the Flood scours the world. Parallel versions survive in the Book of Giants among the Dead Sea Scrolls and in Jubilees; the story circulated widely in the Second Temple period.

Mainstream Jewish and Christian exegesis later favored a tamer reading, taking the 'sons of God' as the line of Seth rather than angels. But the Watchers and their giants never vanished, flowing from medieval legend into modern fantasy fiction, comics, and film, where 'nephilim' is now the standing label for characters of half-angelic blood.

value.zh: 通说释为"坠落者"；七十士译本作"巨人"
《以诺一书》记守望者盟誓于黑门山
value.en: Mount Hermon, where the Watchers swore their oath in 1 Enoch
